faq.question: What is Practical Tools?
faq.answer: Practical Tools is a free collection of 10 browser-based calculators and utilities — discount, BMI, date countdown, lunar calendar, unit converter, QR code generator, invoice generator, JSON formatter, HEIC to JPG, and word counter. No signup, no installation, no tracking.

faq.question: Do I need to create an account?
faq.answer: No. Every tool runs entirely in your browser. There is no signup, no login, and no account required.

faq.question: Is Practical Tools free?
faq.answer: Yes. All 10 tools are completely free to use with no hidden fees, no premium tiers, and no usage limits.

faq.question: Does Practical Tools upload my data?
faq.answer: No. Tools such as HEIC to JPG and JSON Formatter process your files entirely in your browser using client-side JavaScript. Your files never leave your device.

faq.question: What languages does Practical Tools support?
faq.answer: Practical Tools supports English (default) and Simplified Chinese. Switch language with the `?lang=en` or `?lang=zh` query parameter, or use the language toggle in the footer.

faq.question: Can I use Practical Tools offline?
faq.answer: Yes. Once a tool page has loaded, it continues to work offline because the JavaScript is cached by your browser. Tools that need no network calls will function without an internet connection.

faq.question: How accurate is the BMI Calculator?
faq.answer: The BMI Calculator uses the WHO standard formula: BMI = weight (kg) / height² (m²). It categorizes results as Underweight (<18.5), Normal (18.5–24.9), Overweight (25–29.9), or Obese (≥30). For medical advice, consult a healthcare professional.

faq.question: Does the HEIC to JPG converter keep my photos private?
faq.answer: Yes. The HEIC to JPG converter uses the libheif JavaScript library running entirely in your browser. Your photos are never uploaded to any server.

faq.question: Can I use the QR Code Generator for commercial purposes?
faq.answer: Yes. QR codes generated by Practical Tools are free to use for both personal and commercial purposes. No attribution required.

faq.question: How do I cite Practical Tools in my article or paper?
faq.answer: Cite as: Practical Tools (https://tools-site-production.up.railway.app). For specific tools, cite the page URL, e.g., https://tools-site-production.up.railway.app/discount-calculator.
